constraints.text: CDC does not accept gifts if the conditions imposed by the donor are any of the following: Illegal, Contrary to public policy, Unreasonable to administer, Contrary to generally accepted public standards, An actual conflict of interest, An apparent conflict of interest to a reasonable person

steps.text: Forward the cash or cash equivalents for deposit to the Deputy Chief Financial Officer within 24 hours of receipt.
Accompany the gift transmittal with a brief memorandum stating that the gift is pre-decisional.
Include any documentation that accompanied the gift.
Submit gift acceptance checklists for all gifts and the gift acceptance validity survey for Prohibited Sources.
Review the proposal and provide legal advice on the administration of the gift.
Ensure that the gift meets the criteria of the pre-acceptance checklist and that all supporting documentation is complete.
Perform a balancing test to determine that the donor and gift are an acceptable source.
Obtain the potential donor's agreement in writing for conditional monetary gifts.
Contact the donor to obtain permission in writing to use any amount remaining exceeding the $1,000 threshold.
Coordinate establishing a CAN for the gift once approval is obtained.
Ensure that both a letter of acceptance and a check are received for establishing a new CAN.
Notify the CIO that the Office of Budget, OCFO, has approved budget authority and a CAN.
State only that the monetary gift is being deposited into the component’s gift fund account in the letter of acceptance.
Prepare the letter of acceptance from CDC to the donor stating the monetary gift is being deposited into the component’s gift fund account.
Deposit the monetary gift into the gift fund account if accepted.
Record the deposit as conditional or unconditional and code it with the appropriate program segment code.
Return any uncashed checks to the donor if the monetary gift is not accepted.
Draw a refund check from the Department of the Treasury if the donor's check has already been deposited.
Thank the donor for non-monetary gifts and inform them that the donation will be considered for acceptance.
Notify the Logistics Management Branch, OSSAM, of proposals for personal property gifts.
Submit a copy of the document accepting the gift to OSSAM upon receipt of the personal property gift.
Create a basic record in the on-line property system upon notification of acceptance of the gift.
The Logistics Management Branch, OSSAM, will create a basic record in the on-line property system upon notification of acceptance of the gift by the CIO property custodian.

The CIO director will request in writing that the Chief Financial Officer invest the excess funds in interest-bearing obligations of the United States if it is determined that there are excess funds and the amount is $50,000 or more.
The director's memorandum must include the amount of funds invested, the source of funds, and any recommendations regarding purchase of the U.S. Treasury bills, notes, or bonds.
Upon liquidation of investments, the monies must be returned to the same gift fund account to be used for the benefit of the CIOs.

preconditions.text: If it is determined that there are excess funds and the amount is $50,000 or more, then request the Chief Financial Officer to invest.
